macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Nasdaq and Risk Assets Surge Despite US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Risk assets, including the Nasdaq 100, posted a massive post-crisis surge over the past week as markets largely ignored lingering US-Iran questions. The rally raises questions about the conviction behind the gains amid ongoing geopolitical issues.

items.title: Middle East War Revives Stagflation Risks for Global Economy
items.summary: Seven weeks of Middle East conflict are creating uncertainty for the global economic outlook, impacting growth and inflation with stagflation dangers resurfacing. Business surveys from Australia to the US highlight these pressures.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Genius (GENIUS) Token Surges 338% from All-Time Low with 24.93% Daily Gain
items.summary: Genius (GENIUS) posted a 24.93% gain to $0.8536 in the past 24 hours, recovering 338.7% from its all-time low of $0.1948 on April 13, 2026, amid significant on-chain market shifts.[1]

items.title: Over $520 Million in Stablecoins Exit Ethereum Network in 24 Hours
items.summary: More than $520 million in stablecoins were withdrawn from Ethereum in the past 24 hours, potentially impacting liquidity and trading dynamics, with reasons unclear.[2]

items.title: Mysterious Whale Withdraws 32,007 ETH from Binance
items.summary: A whale accumulated 32,007 ETH by withdrawing it from Binance in the last 24 hours, signaling strong buying interest in Ethereum.[3]

items.title: Bitcoin Mining Difficulty Declines Amid Record BTC Sales by Miners
items.summary: Bitcoin mining difficulty decreased on Saturday, highlighting ongoing industry challenges as miners face record sales pressures.[5]

items.title: XRP Expands to Solana Network Sparking New Demand
items.summary: XRP ventures into Solana with a debut, alongside Ripple's ODL corridors expanding for increased cross-border utility, as noted by Ripple CEO.[1]

items.title: AAVE Drops 12% After Potential KelpDAO Liquid Staking Exploit
items.summary: AAVE experienced a 12% price drop following a suspected exploit involving KelpDAO's liquid staking token.[1]

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos AI Model Sparks Alarm in Trump Administration Over Cybersecurity Capabilities
items.summary: Anthropic developed Mythos, a powerful AI model excelling at uncovering and exploiting cybersecurity vulnerabilities, leading to concerns in the Trump administration; the company has withheld public release due to its potency.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7, Faces Backlash for Performance Downgrade
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.7, but users report it consumes 35% more tokens, with long-context retrieval dropping from 78% to 32% on benchmarks, marking it as a perceived downgrade.

items.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Managed Agents; OpenAI and LangChain Respond with Competing Platforms
items.summary: Anthropic introduced Claude Managed Agents, a cloud-hosted platform; LangChain countered with Deep Agents Deploy as an open alternative, and OpenAI updated its Agents SDK.

items.title: Google DeepMind Unveils Advanced AI Models for Weather Forecasting and World Generation
items.summary: Raia Hadsell highlighted DeepMind's GraphCast for 15-day forecasts, GenCast probabilistic model outperforming benchmarks 97% of the time, FGN for cyclone prediction used by US National Hurricane Center, and Genie for interactive 3D worlds.

items.title: Allbirds Abandons Footwear to Reinvent as AI Company
items.summary: Struggling shoe brand Allbirds announced it is exiting the footwear business to pivot entirely to artificial intelligence.

items.title: Catholic Brothers Launch Acutis AI, Faith-Driven Chatbot Based on Catholic Teachings
items.summary: Brothers Peter and Thomas Cooney released Acutis AI, a chatbot grounded in 2,000 years of Catholic tradition with parental controls for ethical, family-safe responses.

items.title: AI Dark Factory Livestream: Autonomous Codebase Generation Goes Live
items.summary: A livestream demonstrated an AI-powered 'Dark Factory' using Archon platform to autonomously write, review, and deploy code for a RAG agent without human intervention.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Fallout 76 Receives New PTS Patch and Tuesday Live Server Update
items.summary: A fresh Public Test Server (PTS) update dropped in the last 24 hours, with an additional live server maintenance patch scheduled for Tuesday.

items.title: Call of Duty: Black Ops 3 PC Gets T7 Patch Update for Safer Clients
items.summary: Following the February 2026 PC update, the T7 patch has been updated to support safer Black Ops 3 clients, though popular mods remain incompatible.

items.title: Crimson Desert Announces Major Patch with Difficulty Settings and Quality Improvements
items.summary: The next significant patch for Crimson Desert includes new difficulty settings, quality enhancements, and additional updates.

items.title: Windrose Early Access Impressions After 24+ Hours of Play
items.summary: Streamers share positive early thoughts on Windrose after extensive playtime in its Early Access phase.

items.title: New Dragon Quest Release and NEO GEO Return Announced This Week
items.summary: Upcoming Dragon Quest title highlighted alongside the revival of NEO GEO, with mentions of Trails series developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Completely Sold Out After Limited Release and Final Restock
items.summary: The Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold, a new tri-foldable smartphone, has completely sold out following its limited release and final restock, indicating strong initial demand.

items.title: Casely Recalling Wireless Power Pods Over Fire and Burn Risks
items.summary: Casely is recalling its wireless power pods due to fire and burn hazards, marking a repeat recall action by the company.

items.title: CPSC Issues Urgent Recall on Over 400,000 Portable Power Banks
items.summary: The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has issued an urgent recall for over 400,000 portable power banks due to safety risks.

items.title: Meta Raises Prices of Quest 3 and Quest 3S VR Headsets Due to Memory Crisis
items.summary: Meta is increasing prices for its Quest 3 and 3S VR headsets, attributed to rising memory chip prices exacerbated by shortages.

items.title: RAM Shortage Could Last for Years, Impacting Tech Industry
items.summary: A ongoing shortage of RAM memory chips is projected to persist for years, affecting pricing and availability across consumer electronics.

items.title: Philips Launches 5K Dual-Mode Gaming Monitor in Western Markets
items.summary: Philips is bringing its new 5K dual-mode gaming monitor, featuring a white shell design, to Western markets.
